# Path-based allowlist for `validate_pool.py` privacy scan.
# One pattern per line. A file is exempted if its path (with both / and \ separators)
# contains any non-comment line below as a substring. Lines starting with `#` and
# blank lines are ignored.
#
# Use this only for files that legitimately contain example credentials, hashes,
# or terms in documentation/teaching contexts. Real leaks must be removed,
# never allowlisted.

# --- Legitimate documentation / teaching examples ---
.github/instructions/security.instructions.md
.github/instructions/mssql-dba.instructions.md
.github/instructions/github-actions.instructions.md
.github/agents/security-analyst.agent.md
.github/skills/cloud/aws-agentic-ai/cross-service/credential-management.md
.github/skills/cloud/aws-serverless-eda/references/security-best-practices.md
.github/skills/cloud/building-mcp-server-on-cloudflare/references/oauth-setup.md
.github/skills/devops/ci-cd-pipeline/SKILL.md
.github/skills/frontend/brand-design/SKILL.md
.github/skills/productivity/jira-issues/SKILL.md
.github/skills/testing/playwright/lib/helpers.js

# --- Pool documentation referencing itself ---
.github/agent-docs/agent-workflow-design-reference.md
.github/agent-docs/PROJECT-ONBOARDING-RUNBOOK.md
.github/agent-docs/RECIPE-RUNBOOK.md
.github/recipes/enterprise-dashboard.recipe.md

# --- Remediation plan deliberately enumerates leaked tokens for cleanup tracking ---
.github/plans/2026-04-27-ai-resource-pool-remediation.md
